decisions.title: Keep spatial, graph, public read-model, and model snapshot projections separate
decisions.decision: Promote reviewed Spatial Event Index records into projections; do not promote one projection into another.
decisions.rationale: Spatial joins and tiling have different correctness requirements than source/provenance relationships and model exports.
must_hold: PostGIS/DuckDB/GeoParquet/PMTiles own spatial projection.
Memgraph/Theseus owns source, claim, conflict, review, and relationship projection.
Public read models exclude private moderation, owner, and raw contributor fields.
Model snapshots cannot mutate public facts.

decisions.title: Represent Lost Flint as source-backed presence intervals
decisions.decision: Use first_seen/last_seen or observed_at semantics for historical buildings and sites unless exact dates are directly sourced.
decisions.rationale: Historical maps, directories, aerials, newspapers, and demolition records rarely support exact lifecycle facts for every building.
must_hold: Do not infer exact demolition dates from absence alone.
Allow approximate or unresolved geometry.
Attach source IDs and rights notes.
Keep model-assisted place links as reviewable proposals.

decisions.title: Track civic actions without inventing causality
decisions.decision: Use promised, funded, work_started, work_completed, outcome_observed, and cancelled_or_changed intervention stages.
decisions.rationale: The atlas should connect public action records to places and outcomes while keeping causal claims source-bounded.
must_hold: Outcome observations can be near an intervention in place/time without being causal claims.
Causality requires explicit source support or a later causal-evidence contract.
Public copy should distinguish promised, funded, work, and observed outcome states.
Funding, agency, project, source, metric, and place relationships belong in the graph projection.

decisions.title: Use MapLibre, Mosaic, and Cosmograph as coordinated surfaces
decisions.decision: MapLibre answers where, Mosaic/vgplot/Observable answers how patterns change, and Cosmograph answers what supports the dossier.
decisions.rationale: The atlas needs geography, analytical crossfiltering, and source/provenance graph exploration without overloading one renderer.
must_hold: Cosmograph is the public-good evidence canvas.
Mosaic/vgplot/Observable Framework is the DuckDB-backed analytical workbench.
MapLibre/PMTiles remains the resident-facing geographic surface.
Do not replace existing UI until a reversible route passes visual gates.

decisions.title: Gate models behind data sufficiency, baselines, uncertainty, and review
decisions.decision: Gemma 4, TimesFM, DyGFormer, higher-order dynamic graph learning, Graph WaveNet, ASTGCN, and road ST-GNN work remain gated until their inputs and public semantics are safe.
decisions.rationale: Open Flint Atlas should be accountable civic science, not model theater.
must_hold: Gemma 4 is late and contest-rule gated.
TimesFM is aggregate trend or benchmark only.
Higher-order dynamic graph learning starts with dynamic civic-knowledge proposals.
Street-safety neural models require road graph, crash aggregates, baselines, fairness, and uncertainty review.
Model outputs are proposals or predictions, not facts.
